expected_properties: When fetch is set to an undefined value (00 or 11), the stored instruction registers retain their previous contents.
Outputs ins, ad1, and ad2 remain unchanged across the clock edge despite changes on the data input.
additional_cases.goal: Confirm that illegal fetch codes do not corrupt internal state or alter observable outputs.
notes: The case tests the module's robustness to undefined control codes while keeping timing assumptions conservative.
preconditions: The module has been initialized by reset_001 and a known instruction value has been loaded via a prior legal fetch.
stimulus_intent: Apply fetch values 2'b00 and 2'b11 with varying data while monitoring that outputs do not change.
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after at least one rising edge of clk following each illegal fetch stimulus.
Do not assume any hidden latency; rely on external output stability.

expected_properties: A random sequence of legal fetch values and data inputs results in outputs that always correspond to the most recently captured instruction according to the fetch code.
No spurious output glitches appear when transitioning between fetch sources.
additional_cases.goal: Stress the instruction register with a mixed pattern of fetch sources to ensure consistent behavior across multiple captures.
notes: The sequence length is kept modest to stay within simulation time while still exercising back‑to‑back captures.
preconditions: Module is out of reset and has completed at least one successful capture.
stimulus_intent: Generate a pseudo‑random series of fetch values (01 or 10) and corresponding data bytes, applying each on successive clock cycles.
timing_assumptions: Each stimulus is applied on a rising edge of clk; outputs are sampled on the following rising edge.
No fixed-cycle latency is assumed beyond the observable one‑cycle capture.

expected_properties: A legal fetch value (01 or 10) causes the corresponding stored instruction register to capture the 8‑bit data input on the next rising edge of clk.
The output 'ins' reflects bits[7:5] of the captured instruction after the capture event.
The output 'ad1' reflects bits[4:0] of the captured instruction when fetch=01.
The output 'ad2' reflects the full 8‑bit instruction when fetch=10.
No change is observed on outputs when fetch holds an undefined value.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Validate that a single legal fetch operation correctly updates the appropriate output fields and that the mapping from stored instruction to outputs follows the specification.
notes: The case remains conservative with respect to latency; observation is performed after at least one clock cycle following the stimulus.
The reset condition is assumed to have been satisfied by reset_001.
stimulus_intent: Drive a legal fetch pattern (01 or 10) together with a representative data byte and verify that the corresponding output fields update accordingly.
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after at least one rising edge of clk following the stimulus.
Do not assume a fixed-cycle latency; rely on externally visible output change.
